title: get_context_window uses substring if/else cascade — OCP violation, no allowlist
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
solid
## Location
src/compaction.rs:166-195 (get_context_window)
## Violation
Model→context-size lookup is a 10-branch `if-else` chain on `model_lower.contains(...)`:

```rust
if model_lower.contains("opus") { CLAUDE_OPUS_CONTEXT }
else if model_lower.contains("sonnet") { CLAUDE_SONNET_CONTEXT }
...
else if model_lower.contains("o1") || model_lower.contains("o3") || model_lower.contains("o4") {
    GPT4O_CONTEXT
} else { DEFAULT_CONTEXT }
```

Problems:
1. Adding a new provider requires editing this function (OCP violation — the rules call for polymorphism or a data table).
2. Substring matching is fragile: `model.contains("gpt-4")` matches `gpt-4o`, `gpt-4-vision`, `gpt-4-turbo`, and any future `gpt-4xxx` accidentally. Today the order saves us (gpt-4o is checked first), but a future model name like `claude-sonnet-gpt-bridge` would silently get sonnet's context window.
3. The default branch silently maps unknown models to `DEFAULT_CONTEXT = 128_000`, which is wrong for `gpt-3.5` if the model name shape changes upstream.
4. Each adapter in providers.rs already owns model metadata — duplicating context-window knowledge here violates SSOT.
## Mandated refactor
1. Move context-window data into a `static MODEL_CONTEXT_TABLE: &[(MatchRule, usize)]` with explicit prefix/suffix/exact rules, ordered most-specific-first.
2. Provide a trait method `ProviderAdapter::context_window(model: &str) -> Option<usize>` and ask the adapter directly.
3. Hard-fail (or log warn + return None) on unknown models so the operator notices, instead of silently using 128k.
4. Add tests: `gpt-4-vision` does not match `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-bridge` does not match gpt-4.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
